paper_title: A diatom-based predictive model for inferring past conductivity in Chadian Sahara lakes

methods.method_name: Field Sampling of Sediments and Water
methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting surface sediment samples and water samples from lakes in the Ounianga region to measure physical and chemical variables. It is used to gather data necessary for understanding the hydrology and salinity of the lakes.

steps.description: Collect a surface sediment sample from the sediment/water interface (topmost < 1 cm) by scraping if the lake was shallow, or with a grab-sampler if the lake was deep and required use of a boat.
steps.searchable_summary: collect surface sediment samples from lakes using appropriate tools

steps.description: Collect water to measure physical and chemical variables.
steps.searchable_summary: collect water samples for physical and chemical analysis

steps.description: When conditions in the field permitted access, collect diatoms in surface water using a phytoplankton net with 5 μm mesh.
steps.searchable_summary: collect diatoms using a phytoplankton net

steps.description: Sample aquatic vegetation by scraping, using a spatula; in some cases, collect a piece of vegetation, to be dealt with later, in the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: collect aquatic vegetation samples using a spatula
